title: Anti-Cheat Research
description: Understanding anti-cheat architecture for defensive security research. Educational focus only — no bypass instructions.

topics.title: User-Mode Anti-Cheats
topics.explanation:
User-mode AC runs in process or as separate service:
- Module enumeration
- Memory integrity checks
- Window/process scanning
- Hook detection on game modules
- Screenshot/hash verification

Research goal: understand attack surface for defensive hardening.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for User-Mode Anti-Cheats
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining User-Mode Anti-Cheats in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Kernel-Mode Anti-Cheats
topics.explanation:
Kernel AC via signed drivers:
- ObRegisterCallbacks (handle stripping)
- PsSetCreateProcessNotifyRoutine
- Image load notify callbacks
- Minifilter for file integrity
- IOCTL heartbeat with usermode component

Study in VM with driver logging.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Kernel-Mode Anti-Cheats
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Kernel-Mode Anti-Cheats in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Integrity Checks
topics.explanation:
Techniques:
- CRC/hash of code sections
- Canary values in data
- Periodic re-scanning
- Server-side validation

Defense: server authority, encrypted protocols, attestation.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Integrity Checks
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Integrity Checks in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Driver Communication
topics.explanation:
Common patterns:
- IOCTL via DeviceIoControl
- Shared memory sections
- Filter communication ports
- Named pipes

Analyze with IRP logging in WinDbg (research VMs).
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Driver Communication
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Driver Communication in your own words

Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Detection Techniques Overview
topics.description: Catalog of detection methods for researchers.
topics.explanation:
Categories:
- **Behavioral** — impossible actions, statistics
- **Environmental** — known tools, debugger presence
- **Integrity** — modified code/memory
- **Network** — anomalous packets
- **Hardware** — HWID tracking

Focus: how to *design* secure systems, not evade them.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Detection Techniques Overview
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Detection Techniques Overview in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic
